purpose: *** IMPLEMENTATION risks — the ways THIS PROGRAM can go wrong. Distinct from kb/risks.json, which records ARCHITECTURAL risks (the ways the SYSTEM is fragile). A slice that ships correctly and still breaks the build is an implementation risk. A slice that ships correctly and destroys data is an architectural one that the contract failed to fence. ***

risks.title: *** A SLICE HOISTS A LAZY IMPORT AND THE PROCESS STOPS STARTING ***
risks.severity: *** MEDIUM-HIGH — and it is the risk that makes EVERY OTHER SLICE dangerous. ***
risks.mechanism: Cycle 5 (whole-tree AST): 107 of 323 lazy edges point to an equal-or-higher level; *** 56 are STRICTLY UPWARD. *** The SCC-condensed compile graph is an 11-level DAG ONLY BECAUSE those imports are deferred to call time. `config` — LEVEL 0, FAN-IN 82, the most-depended-on module in the system — reaches UP to `accounts` (L2) and `meta_graph` (L3).
risks.why_it_is_LIVE_for_this_program: *** S02 TOUCHES `accounts`. S07 touches `pipeline`. S08/S09 touch `cli` and `doctor`. And NOT ONE OF CYCLE 4'S TEN PROMPTS MENTIONS IT — because Cycle 5's dependency model DID NOT EXIST when they were written. ***
risks.the_trap: *** Hoisting one of the 56 is a change that LOOKS LIKE A CLEANUP AND THAT A REVIEWER WOULD WAVE THROUGH. It breaks the process at start. There is no test, no lint rule, and no layer declaration that would catch it. ***
mitigation: GB-1 (a MANDATORY boundary on every slice)
S12 (PROPOSED — makes it mechanical; gated on PD-5)
risks.residual_if_S12_is_rejected: *** GB-1 survives as a REVIEW obligation rather than a CI gate. That is strictly weaker, and it is the state the repository has been in for its entire life. Survivable — but it is exactly the shape ('a property protected by attention, not by a mechanism') that produced INV-01, INV-02, INV-03 and AR-03. ***

risks.title: A slice deletes live code because a call graph says 'zero callers'
risks.mechanism: *** COUP-10: `post/providers.py` dispatches SIX backend factories via lazy in-function import LAMBDAS from a dict. A NAME-BASED CALL GRAPH FLAGS ALL SIX AS UNCALLED. ALL SIX ARE LIVE. *** Same trap: `post.compress.persist_post_shrink` (lazy-imported from studio/actions.py).
risks.mitigation: GB-2 — *** 'zero callers' is a LEAD, NEVER a verdict. Any deletion must ship an AST census over the WHOLE tree, not a grep. ***

risks.title: A slice 'tightens' a ledger model to extra='forbid' and bricks a forward-rolled ledger
risks.mechanism: Forward-compat (INV-19 / SHIM-005) holds by pydantic's DEFAULT (`extra='ignore'`), NOT by declaration. Setting `extra='forbid'` turns a forward-rolled ledger into a hard `ControlFileError` and bricks EVERY reader.
slices_at_risk: *** S02 — its REJECTED Option C was EXACTLY this (a typed enum on Account.backends). ***
S11 (it is ABOUT the validator boundary and an agent may 'strengthen' it)
mitigation: S02's `test_per_row_leniency_survives` PRESERVATION test — *** which makes the rejection of Option C MECHANICAL rather than a paragraph in a prompt. ***

risks.title: *** THE cli.py PRINT-COUNT COLLISION — three slices, one exact-equality constant ***
risks.mechanism: *** `tests/test_internal_prints_routed.py` asserts `len(print_calls(cli.py)) == 147` — EXACT EQUALITY, NOT A CEILING. S08, S09 AND S10 ALL TOUCH cli.py. If two land carrying a bumped constant, the second's rebase yields a WRONG count and CI goes RED for a reason unrelated to its change. ***
risks.discovery: *** DERIVED_FACT — read from the test file at fcffa73. Cycle 4 named the ratchet ('replay both AST ratchets') but never derived the PER-SLICE BUDGET and never noticed the constant is SHARED and EXACT. ***
risks.mitigation: *** THE RULE: NO SLICE MAY CHANGE cli.py's print() COUNT. Route new operator output through `get_logger`, or reuse an existing print. This is FREE — none of S08/S09/S10 NEEDS a new print — and it DISSOLVES the coupling rather than serializing around it. ***
risks.escape_hatch: If a slice PROVES it must: at most ONE open PR may modify `_CLI_PRINT_COUNT`, with a rationale comment (the file's existing convention). Cycle 7 rejects a second concurrent PR touching that constant.
risks.sibling: *** `ledger.py` and `pipeline.py` are in `_INTERNAL_MODULES` — they must contain ZERO print(). S01 owns ledger.py; S07 owns pipeline.py. NEITHER MAY ADD ONE. ***

risks.title: S06 and S09 collide textually in studio/actions.py
risks.mechanism: S06 edits :944-951 (`bulk_send_to_review`). S09 edits :1029-1034 and :1100-1102 (the two retry paths). ~80 lines apart, different functions, no shared state.
risks.mitigation: *** file_ownership.json PARTITIONS THE FILE BY FUNCTION, not by line. A widening is detectable without reading either diff. A rebase resolves the text; the CONTRACT resolves the scope. ***

risks.title: *** S01's TEST REWRITE IS READ AS THE REGRESSION AND THE FIX IS REJECTED ***
risks.severity: *** CRITICAL — and it is a HUMAN risk, not a technical one. ***
risks.mechanism: *** `test_restore_snapshot_serializes_with_transaction` currently ASSERTS THE DATA-LOSS OUTCOME and PASSES *BECAUSE OF* IT. Its comment names a mechanism that does not exist ('restorer blocked on flock' — measured: 0.001s, never blocked). ANY CORRECT FIX TURNS IT RED. A maintainer seeing a green test go red will read THE FIX AS THE REGRESSION. ***
mitigation: *** The rewrite MUST ship in the SAME PR as the source fix (MUST_SHIP_ATOMICALLY). ***
*** The independent verifier MUST be briefed on RC-5 BEFORE reviewing. This is a NAMED APPROVAL PRECONDITION, not a courtesy. ***
The PR description must say so, loudly, at the top.
risks.note: *** This is the only risk in the register whose failure mode is a CORRECT FIX BEING REJECTED. ***

risks.title: *** S07 'UNIFIES THE GATING' AND ARMS IRREVERSIBLE MOMENT RETIREMENT ***
risks.severity: *** CRITICAL — the worst outcome the program can produce. ***
risks.mechanism: *** S07 removes the `is_live_backend` gate from `_reconcile_safe` (pipeline.py:318). The SAME predicate gates `_learn_pass` (cli.py:965) — which calls `adjust.retire`, which writes `MomentState.retired`, which `reconcile_moments` REFUSES TO UNDO. IT IS STRUCTURALLY IRREVERSIBLE. 'Unifying the gating' is THE MOST NATURAL-LOOKING CLEANUP IN THAT DIFF, and it would run PERMANENT MOMENT RETIREMENT on a deployment where learning was previously frozen. ***
risks.compounding: *** RC-7 / PD-3 IS UNRESOLVED. Nobody has even decided whether that retirement policy is CORRECT. ***
mitigation: *** `test_learn_pass_is_still_gated_on_is_live_backend` — a CONTRACT test. THE CHEAPEST POSSIBLE GUARD AGAINST THE PROGRAM'S WORST OUTCOME. WRITE IT. ***
The independent verifier for S07 MUST specifically confirm the gate is untouched — it is written into the approval requirement, not left to notice.
S07's post-rollback validation checks the gate SURVIVED THE REVERT.
risks.live_exposure: *** ZERO moments retired today (LS-2). The actuator has never fired. That LIMITS the damage of a mistake — it does not excuse one. ***

risks.title: A slice adds a FIFTH door to the R1 poison pill
risks.mechanism: *** The R1 published-URL invariant is enforced AT CONSTRUCTION ONLY. `model_copy(update=)` and `setattr` BOTH bypass every validator. The property survives via FOUR INDEPENDENT MANUAL CALL-SITE GUARDS. A FIFTH DOOR ADDED WITHOUT ONE SAVES CLEANLY AND THEN BRICKS THE NEXT `Ledger.load` — taking down the daemon and every Studio page at once. ***
risks.why_it_is_LIVE_for_this_program: *** S03, S04 AND S06 ALL MUTATE `Post`. THREE SLICES, EACH TOUCHING THE EXACT MODEL WITH THE POISON PILL. ***
mitigation: *** S11 — which is WHY the recommended landing order puts it SECOND, before S03/S04/S06. It is the difference between 'we told them not to' and 'CI will not let them'. ***

risks.title: S04 lands and its ladder never runs, because S07 has not
risks.severity: MEDIUM (a silent no-op, not a break)
risks.mechanism: *** If `_reconcile_safe` is gated OFF (is_live_backend False), S04's terminal ladder NEVER EXECUTES. The slice is CORRECT and UNREACHABLE. INT-2 holds only once BOTH land. ***
risks.mitigation: *** Modelled explicitly as a CO-REQUIREMENT (not an ordering edge — that would manufacture a DAG cycle that does not exist). The completion matrix marks INT-2 INCOMPLETE until both are merged. ***

risks.title: *** THIS CONTRACT BECOMES THE NEXT INV-20 ***
risks.class: *** meta — and it is the one the codebase's own history predicts. ***
risks.mechanism: *** This repository's SIGNATURE DEFECT, found in every one of five cycles, is: 'THE DOC NAMES A MECHANISM THAT DOES NOT EXIST, WHILE THE PROPERTY SURVIVES VIA A DIFFERENT ONE.' It has appeared in the docs (INV-01/02/03/05/07), in the CODE COMMENTS (4 for 4), and — most dangerously — IN THE TEST LAYER (AR-03/RC-5). *** THIS CONTRACT IS A DOC. It is the next candidate. ***
risks.the_specific_failure: A future engineer reads 'S02 owns accounts.py' and trusts it, while a sixth slice has quietly started editing accounts.py. The contract SAYS the boundary exists. NOTHING ENFORCES IT.
mitigation: *** EVERY boundary in this contract is expressed as a MACHINE-CHECKABLE PREDICATE — a file path, a function name, a numeric baseline, an AST assertion — and NOT as prose. That is why file_ownership.json exists and why the prose doc is the twin, not the source. ***
*** Cycle 7's ENTIRE JOB is to make these mechanical. A contract that ships without Cycle 7 is a promise, and this codebase has taught us exactly what a promise is worth. ***
*** The two AST ratchets already in CI are the PROOF that this repository CAN enforce a policy mechanically. They are the model. ***
risks.honest_statement: *** Until Cycle 7 lands, this contract is enforced by ATTENTION. That is better than nothing and it is NOT a mechanism. Recorded here rather than hoped away. ***

risks.title: The live-state measurements go stale between contract freeze and merge
risks.mechanism: *** THREE risks in this program were COLLAPSED by measuring the live tree on 2026-07-14: S04's blast radius (0 rows), PD-3's accrued cost (0 retired), and the S02<->S10 hazard (0 malformed backends). *** All three are properties of a MUTABLE tree. The operator can approve a post, hand-edit accounts.json, or run a learn pass at any time.
risks.mitigation: *** EVERY ONE OF THE THREE IS RE-ARMED AS A MERGE GATE. The slice's PR must RE-RUN the check and STATE the result. A collapsed risk that is not re-verified at merge is a risk that was merely NOT LOOKED AT. ***

the_probes.S02: parse 00_control/accounts.json; assert every backends[platform] value is in {dryrun, postiz, zernio}.
the_probes.S04: count posts in {submitting, submitted, needs_reconcile}. If > 0, run the report-only mode and paste its output.

risks.title: OPS-001: the program is designed and reviewed single-threaded
risks.mechanism: *** The orchestration gate has refused every subagent spawn for SIX CONSECUTIVE CYCLES. No independent agent was able to REFUTE this contract's boundaries. ***
risks.mitigation: *** Cycle 6 grounded every COLLAPSIBLE risk in a DIRECT MEASUREMENT of the live tree rather than in an argument. A measurement does not need a second opinion in the way an inference does. The boundaries that remain INFERRED (GB-1..GB-5) are inherited from Cycles 2-5, each independently derived. ***
risks.residual: *** The SYNTHESIS — this contract — has never been adversarially reviewed. Its individual claims have. ***
risks.disengage: `orchestrate.py stop` — AN OPERATOR ACTION, not a code change.

risk_ranking_by_what_it_actually_COSTS: *** IR-7 (S07 arms irreversible retirement) — the worst OUTCOME. One CONTRACT test prevents it. ***
*** IR-6 (S01's correct fix is rejected because a green test goes red) — the worst PROCESS failure, and it blocks the only CRITICAL slice. ***
*** IR-1 (a lazy-import hoist breaks the process at start) — the risk that makes EVERY OTHER SLICE dangerous, and the one Cycle 4 could not have known about. ***
IR-8 (a fifth door to the poison pill) — LOW probability, CATASTROPHIC blast radius. S11 makes it mechanical.
IR-10 (the contract becomes doc-drift) — MEDIUM, and it is the failure this codebase's history most strongly predicts.
IR-11 (the live measurements decay) — MEDIUM. Fully mitigated by re-arming each as a merge gate.
IR-3, IR-2 — MEDIUM, both fenced by a GLOBAL BOUNDARY.
IR-4, IR-5, IR-9 — merge friction and silent no-ops. Annoying, not dangerous.
IR-12 — process. Recorded, mitigated by measurement, not resolved.
